name: time_squeeze_range_rejection_cases
description: AC-6 (M-1): compute_time_squeeze_decay must raise an explicit ValueError on time_ratio outside [0,1], rather than crashing with an opaque log10 domain error (time_ratio < -1/9 makes 1+9t<=0) or silently over-tightening the stop (time_ratio > 1). Endpoints 0.0 and 1.0 are valid and must still yield the documented MULT_OPEN/MULT_CLOSE and MIN_STOP_OPEN/MIN_STOP_CLOSE values.
derivation: Valid domain is the closed interval [0,1]. time_ratio < 0: out of range (and < -1/9 specifically triggers a math.log10 domain crash on the live execution path). time_ratio > 1: out of range, over-tightens below MULT_CLOSE/MIN_STOP_CLOSE. The function must validate and raise BEFORE the log10 call. Endpoint cases assert the exact documented values: at t=0 decay_curve=log10(1)=0 -> MULT_OPEN=1.5, MIN_STOP_OPEN=0.3; at t=1 decay_curve=log10(10)=1 -> MULT_CLOSE=0.5, MIN_STOP_CLOSE=0.15.
